docs.title: VATSIM Pakistan Library
docs.text:
Welcome to the official VATSIM Pakistan Library.

This library contains operational documents, policies, airport briefings, and pilot/controller resources for flying and controlling within Pakistani airspace on VATSIM.

docs.title: Quick Access
docs.text:
• Policies
  • Airport Briefings
  • Aerodrome Guides
  • Karachi / OPKC Briefing
  • Lahore / OPLA Briefing
  • Islamabad / OPIS Briefing

!!! Note: This library is maintained for simulation use on the VATSIM network only.

docs.title: Aerodrome Guides
docs.text: Aerodrome guides provide operational guidance and recommended VATSIM procedures for major Pakistani airports. These guides are intended to help both pilots and controllers understand local operations, preferred runway usage, taxi procedures, SID usage, VFR operations, and general aerodrome practices commonly used within the VATSIM Pakistan FIR.

docs.title: Southern Pakistan
docs.text: • Karachi / OPKC Jinnah International Airport guide covering runway operations, taxi procedures, RNAV departures, VFR traffic handling, and local ATC practices.

docs.title: Central Pakistan
docs.text: • Lahore / OPLA Allama Iqbal International Airport guide including preferred runway operations, low visibility procedures, circuit operations, and departure handling.

docs.title: Northern Pakistan
docs.text:
• Islamabad / OPIS Islamabad International Airport guide covering CAT III operations, terrain-sensitive procedures, VFR circuit operations, and standard IFR departures.

!!! info These aerodrome guides are designed exclusively for flight simulation use on the VATSIM network and must not be used for real-world aviation or navigation purposes.

!!! tip Pilots are encouraged to review the relevant aerodrome guide before connecting to VATSIM Pakistan to ensure familiarity with local procedures and ATC expectations.

!!! warning Controllers may deviate from procedures outlined in these guides due to traffic, weather, events, or operational requirements.

docs.title: 2. Departure Clearance Procedures
docs.text:
Delivery Controller is responsible for validating routes and providing IFR clearance to departing aircraft. The controller shall assign the correct departure procedure based on the first point filed in the flight plan.

Where the flight plan contains an invalid route, level, or departure procedure, Delivery Controller shall ensure the error is corrected before clearance is issued.

docs.title: 2.1 Requesting ATC Clearance
docs.text:
Pilots are expected to report the following on first contact before requesting ATC clearance:

  1. Callsign
  2. Stand number
  3. Destination
  4. Current ATIS letter
  5. Requested flight level

docs.title: 2.2 Receiving ATC Clearance
docs.text:
An ATC clearance shall contain the following:

  1. Callsign
  2. Destination
  3. Departure procedure / Radar Vectors / SID / Airway
  4. Runway in use
  5. Initial flight level
  6. Assigned SSR code

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Pilot: Islamabad Delivery, PIA301 at stand 12, destination Karachi, requested level FL380, we have information C, requesting ATC clearance.

Controller: PIA301, Islamabad Delivery, cleared to destination Karachi enroute J121, INDEK2C departure, RWY 10R in use, initially climb and maintain altitude 8000 ft, expect higher enroute, squawk on departure 3642.

Pilot: Cleared to destination enroute J121 via the INDEK2C departure, RWY 10R, climb initially altitude 8000 ft, squawk 3642, PIA301.

Controller: PIA301, readback is correct, information C is current, report ready for push and start.

Note: QNH must be provided during taxi instructions.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, taxi to holding point RWY 10R via M,T,K,A, QNH 1013.

docs.title: 2.3.1 RNAV Instrument Departures
docs.text:
Islamabad generally uses RNAV SIDs for departures and these are preferred. Certain non-RNAV SIDs may also be used.

Commonly used RNAV SIDs:

  1. BELKO2F
  2. DAMTO2G
  3. INDEK2D
  4. KALMI2D
  5. INDEK2C

docs.title: 2.3.2 Omni-directional Departures
docs.text:
When a pilot is unable to fly a SID, an omni-directional departure may be assigned. Aircraft can expect radar vectors to the first waypoint filed in the flight plan.

During takeoff clearance, Tower shall assign an initial heading. Further vectors will then be issued by Approach.

docs.title: Example ATC Clearance
docs.text:
Pilot: Islamabad Delivery As-salamu alaykum, PIA301, stand 12, POB 112, requested level 380, request IFR clearance to Karachi, we are unable to accept SID.

Controller: PIA301, cleared to destination via the omni-directional departure, RWY 28L in use, initially climb and maintain altitude 8000 ft, squawk 3642.

docs.title: Example Takeoff Clearance
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, after departure turn right heading 010, surface winds 260 degrees 08 knots, RWY 28L, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 2.4 Runway Change
docs.text:
At Islamabad, the primary runway in use is RWY 28L due to the availability of CAT II and CAT III ILS systems which support low visibility procedures (LVP).

Aircraft already cleared shall receive updated clearances reflecting the revised runway and SID.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Controller: PIA301, runway has been changed. ATC clearance available, report ready to copy.

Pilot: Ready to copy clearance, PIA301.

Controller: PIA301 re-cleared to destination via the INDEK2D departure, RWY 28L in use, initially climb to altitude 8000 ft, squawk 3642.

docs.title: 2.5.1 General
docs.text:
VFR operations at OPIS are permitted subject to ATC clearance and prevailing traffic conditions. Due to high IFR traffic volume at Islamabad International Airport, VFR aircraft may be delayed, rerouted, or subject to altitude restrictions.

All VFR aircraft shall maintain continuous two-way radio communication with ATC while operating within controlled airspace.

docs.title: 2.5.2 Airspace Classification
docs.text:
The airspace surrounding OPIS is controlled airspace.

Therefore:

  • VFR flights require explicit ATC clearance before entry.
  • Standard VFR “see and avoid” principles apply.
  • ATC shall provide separation from IFR traffic where possible.
  • Pilots remain responsible for maintaining vigilance for other traffic.

docs.title: 2.5.3 VFR Entry and Exit Procedures
docs.text:
VFR aircraft shall use designated reporting points when entering or exiting the Islamabad CTR.

Pilots must report:

  1. Position
  2. Altitude
  3. Intentions

ATC may assign:

  1. Specific routing
  2. Altitude restrictions
  3. Holding instructions if required

docs.title: 2.5.4 Circuit Procedures
docs.text:
Standard traffic circuits at OPIS are conducted as follows unless otherwise instructed:

Item Information Circuit Altitude Typically 1000 ft AGL (approximately 2800 ft) Circuit Direction Left-hand or right-hand depending on runway in use Circuit Area Always south of the airfield Joining Procedure Overhead join or as instructed by ATC

Pilots shall:

  1. Report joining, downwind, base, and final
  2. Maintain spacing from preceding traffic
  3. Be prepared for extended circuits during busy traffic periods

docs.title: 3. Ground Movement Procedures
docs.text:
Ground Movement Controller (GMC) is responsible for all aircraft movements on aerodrome movement areas except runways and associated taxiways.

Departing aircraft are provided pushback and taxi instructions. Arriving aircraft are assigned stands and taxi routes.

For intersection departure requests, Ground Controller shall coordinate with Tower before approval.

docs.title: 3.1 Pushback Procedures
docs.text:
Aircraft requesting pushback without displaying the assigned transponder code shall remain on stand until the correct squawk is set.

Pushback or ground movement shall not be authorized until compliance is confirmed.

Depending on stand allocation, aircraft shall receive a specific pushback direction.

docs.title: 3.1.2 Coordination Requirement
docs.text: Where pushback affects taxiway flow or sequencing, Ground Controller shall coordinate appropriately with adjacent positions.

docs.title: 3.2.1 Departure Taxi Clearance
docs.text:
• Aircraft shall receive taxi instructions to the appropriate holding point after pushback and engine start.
  • Taxi routes shall be issued in full where necessary.
  • Pilots shall read back all taxi instructions.

docs.title: 3.2.2 Taxi Route Management
docs.text:
• Taxi instructions shall prioritize efficiency and safety.
  • Intermediate holding points may be used to regulate traffic.
  • Aircraft shall exercise caution at intersections and give way when instructed.

docs.title: 3.2.3 Runway Coordination and Holding Points
docs.text:
• Aircraft shall not enter holding points without Tower clearance.
  • GMC shall hand aircraft to Tower at appropriate positions to avoid unnecessary stopping.

docs.title: 3.2.4 Taxiways for All Runways
docs.text: Taxiway routing shall be assigned by Ground Controller based on stand allocation, active runway, and prevailing traffic conditions.

docs.title: Main Terminal
docs.text: Stand Range Usage Stands 13–28 Domestic Flights Stands 1–12 International Flights

docs.title: 3.3.1 Restrictions
docs.text:
• Stands 6, 7, and 8 are certified for Super category aircraft operations including the A380.
  • Remaining stands support heavy to medium aircraft.
  • Stands 13–16, 25–28, 1–3, and 10–12 are remote stands without direct boarding infrastructure.

docs.title: 3.4 Handoff Procedures
docs.text:
Transfer of control between ATS units shall ensure continuous separation and situational awareness.

Controllers shall not issue clearances beyond their area of responsibility without coordination.

Aircraft may be instructed to hold at suitable intermediate points when required.

docs.title: 4. Aerodrome Procedures
docs.text:
The Aerodrome Controller (ADC) is responsible for all movements on active runways and associated taxiways.

ADC shall ensure safe and efficient IFR arrival and departure separation while also coordinating IFR and VFR operations within the control zone.

docs.title: 4.2 Preferred Runways
docs.text:
Due to CAT III ILS capability, RWY 28L is the preferred runway.

When wind conditions require a runway change combined with wind changes greater than 8 knots, RWY 10R becomes the preferred runway.

docs.title: 4.3.1 Line-up Clearance
docs.text: Aircraft handed over to Tower may be cleared to line up once the runway is confirmed clear.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, via Holding Point J, line up and wait RWY 28L.

docs.title: Conditional Line-up Clearance
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, behind departing company A320, line up and wait RWY 28L behind.

docs.title: 4.3.2 Takeoff Clearance
docs.text: Aircraft shall be cleared for takeoff once separation standards are met.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, surface winds 260 degrees 10 knots, RWY 28L, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 4.3.3 Separation Requirements
docs.text:
• IFR departures shall comply with standard IFR separation minima.
  • Enhanced wake turbulence separation (eWTS) shall be applied where applicable.
  • Aircraft on the same SID shall generally receive a minimum spacing of two minutes unless reduced separation is permitted.
  • VFR departures may be cleared maintaining visual separation where appropriate.

docs.title: 4.3.4 Departure Handoff Procedure
docs.text: Aircraft shall normally be handed to Departure after becoming airborne and reaching 1000 ft AGL.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, airborne time 17, contact Islamabad Departures 119.650.

docs.title: 4.3.5 Omni-directional Departures
docs.text: Aircraft conducting omni-directional departures may receive heading assignments after departure to assist with sequencing and terrain separation.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, after departure fly heading 270, maintain altitude 2000 ft, wind 280 degrees 6 knots, RWY 28L, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 4.3.6 Rejecting a Departure
docs.text: Aircraft may be instructed to reject takeoff where required to prevent unsafe situations.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, cancel takeoff clearance, stop immediately, I say again, stop immediately.

docs.title: 4.4 Arrival Procedures
docs.text: While Approach primarily manages arrival separation, ADC shall ensure separation is maintained until the preceding aircraft crosses the threshold safely.

docs.title: 4.4.1 Speed Restriction
docs.text: ADC may issue speed control instructions to maintain arrival spacing.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA301, surface winds 270 degrees 08 knots, RWY 28L, continue approach number 2, reduce to minimum approach speed for separation.

docs.title: 4.4.2 Visual Separation
docs.text: Aircraft without Mode C or VFR aircraft may be instructed to maintain visual separation.

docs.title: 4.4.3 Go-around Instruction
docs.text: Controllers may issue go-around instructions where landing is no longer suitable.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Controller: PIA301, go around, I say again, go around, acknowledge going around.

Pilot: PIA301 going around.

Controller: PIA301, continue missed approach procedure, contact Islamabad Departures 119.650.

docs.title: 4.4.4 Arrival Taxi Procedure
docs.text: After vacating the runway, aircraft shall receive taxi instructions and be transferred to Ground once fully clear of the runway environment.

docs.title: 4.5 VFR Procedures
docs.text: ADC is responsible for managing all VFR traffic within the control zone.

docs.title: 4.5.1 VFR Departures
docs.text:
Once ready and traffic conditions permit, VFR aircraft shall be cleared for departure in an orderly sequence.

Aircraft shall report when leaving the control zone.

docs.title: 4.5.2 VFR Traffic in Circuit
docs.text:
At Islamabad, circuit operations are conducted south of the airfield.

Aircraft shall:

  • Enter downwind as instructed
  • State intentions
  • Expect orbit instructions during IFR arrival sequencing where required

Once orbiting is no longer required, ADC shall issue base turn instructions followed by landing or touch-and-go clearance.

docs.title: 4.5.3 VFR Arrivals
docs.text:
Inbound VFR aircraft shall establish contact with ADC before entering the control zone.

ADC shall issue:

  • Joining instructions
  • Circuit direction
  • Circuit altitude
  • Runway in use
  • Traffic information
  • Sequencing requirements

Pilots shall remain outside controlled airspace until clearance to enter has been issued.

docs.title: 2. Departure Clearance Procedures
docs.text:
Ground Controller is responsible for validating routes and providing IFR clearance to departing aircraft. The controller shall assign the correct departure procedure based on the first point filed in the flight plan.

Where the flight plan contains an invalid route, level, or departure procedure, Ground Controller shall ensure that the error is corrected before clearance is issued.

docs.title: 2.1 Requesting ATC Clearance
docs.text:
Pilots are expected to report the following on first contact before requesting ATC clearance:

  1. Callsign
  2. Stand number
  3. Destination
  4. Current ATIS letter
  5. Requested flight level

docs.title: 2.2 Receiving ATC Clearance
docs.text:
An ATC clearance shall contain the following:

  1. Callsign
  2. Destination
  3. Departure procedure / Radar Vectors / SID / Airway
  4. Runway in use
  5. Initial flight level
  6. Assigned SSR code

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Pilot: Lahore Ground, PIA304 at stand 12, destination Karachi, requested level FL380, we have information C, requesting ATC clearance.

Controller: PIA304, Lahore Ground, cleared to destination Karachi enroute G214, MIMAL2D departure, RWY 36R in use, initially climb and maintain FL070, expect higher enroute, squawk on departure 3642.

Pilot: Cleared to destination enroute G214 via the MIMAL2D departure, RWY 36R, climb initially FL070, squawk 3642, PIA304.

Controller: PIA304, readback is correct, information C is current, report ready for push and start.

Note: QNH must be given during taxi instructions.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, taxi to holding point RWY 36R via P,Q, QNH 1013.

docs.title: 2.3.1 RNAV Instrument Departures
docs.text:
Lahore generally uses RNAV SIDs for departures and these are preferred. Certain non-RNAV SIDs may also be used.

Commonly used RNAV SIDs:

  1. MIMAL2D
  2. BIBUN2D
  3. OMUKI2D
  4. RABAN2D
  5. VEREN1D

docs.title: 2.3.2 Omni-directional Departures
docs.text:
When a pilot is unable to fly a SID, an omni-directional departure may be assigned. The aircraft can expect radar vectors to the first waypoint filed in the flight plan.

During takeoff clearance, Tower shall assign a heading to follow after departure. Further vectors will then be issued by Approach.

docs.title: Example ATC Clearance
docs.text:
Pilot: Lahore Ground As-salamu alaykum, PIA304, stand 12, POB 112, requested level 380, request IFR clearance to Karachi, we are unable to accept SID.

Controller: PIA304, cleared to destination via the omni-directional departure, RWY 36R in use, initially climb and maintain FL070, squawk 3642.

docs.title: Example Takeoff Clearance
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, after departure turn left heading 300, surface winds 290 degrees 08 knots, RWY 36R, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 2.4 Runway Change
docs.text:
The primary runway in use at Lahore is RWY 36R due to the availability of CAT II and CAT III ILS systems which support low visibility procedures (LVP).

If a runway change occurs, aircraft already cleared shall be re-cleared with the updated runway and SID.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Controller: PIA304, runway has been changed. ATC clearance available, report ready to copy.

Pilot: Ready to copy clearance, PIA304.

Controller: PIA304 re-cleared to destination via the MIMAL2E departure, RWY 18L in use, initially climb to FL070, squawk 3642.

docs.title: 2.5.1 General
docs.text:
VFR operations at OPLA are permitted subject to ATC clearance and prevailing traffic conditions. Due to high IFR traffic volume, VFR aircraft may be delayed, rerouted, or subject to altitude restrictions.

All VFR aircraft shall maintain continuous two-way radio communication with ATC while operating within controlled airspace.

docs.title: 2.5.2 Airspace Classification
docs.text:
The airspace surrounding OPLA is controlled airspace.

Therefore:

  • VFR flights require explicit ATC clearance before entry.
  • Standard VFR “see and avoid” principles apply.
  • ATC shall provide separation from IFR traffic where possible.
  • Pilots remain responsible for maintaining vigilance for other traffic.

docs.title: 2.5.3 VFR Entry and Exit Procedures
docs.text:
VFR aircraft shall use designated reporting points when entering or exiting the Lahore CTR.

Pilots must report:

  1. Position
  2. Altitude
  3. Intentions

ATC may assign:

  1. Specific routing
  2. Altitude restrictions
  3. Holding instructions if required

docs.title: 2.5.4 Circuit Procedures
docs.text:
Standard traffic circuits at OPLA are conducted as follows unless otherwise instructed:

Item Information Circuit Altitude 1000 ft AGL (approximately 1700 ft) Circuit Direction Left-hand or right-hand depending on runway in use Circuit Area Always west of the airfield due to localizer presence east of the field Joining Procedure Overhead join or as instructed by ATC

Pilots shall:

  1. Report joining, downwind, base, and final
  2. Maintain spacing from preceding traffic
  3. Be prepared for extended circuits during busy traffic periods

docs.title: 3. Ground Movement Procedures
docs.text:
Ground Movement Controller (GMC) is responsible for all aircraft movements on aerodrome movement areas except runways and associated taxiways.

Departing aircraft are provided pushback and taxi instructions. Arriving aircraft are assigned stands and taxi routes.

For intersection departure requests, Ground Controller shall coordinate with Tower before approval.

docs.title: 3.1 Pushback Procedures
docs.text:
Aircraft requesting pushback without displaying the assigned transponder code shall remain on stand until the correct squawk is set.

Pushback or ground movement shall not be authorized until compliance is confirmed.

Depending on stand allocation, aircraft shall receive a specific pushback direction.

docs.title: 3.1.2 Coordination Requirement
docs.text: Where pushback affects taxiway flow or sequencing, Ground Controller shall coordinate appropriately with adjacent positions.

docs.title: 3.2.1 Departure Taxi Clearance
docs.text:
• Aircraft shall receive taxi instructions to the appropriate holding point after pushback and engine start.
  • Taxi routes shall be issued in full where necessary.
  • Pilots shall read back all taxi instructions.

docs.title: 3.2.2 Taxi Route Management
docs.text:
• Taxi instructions shall prioritize efficiency and safety.
  • Intermediate holding points may be used to regulate traffic.
  • Aircraft shall exercise caution at intersections and give way when instructed.

docs.title: 3.2.3 Runway Coordination and Holding Points
docs.text:
• Aircraft shall not enter holding points without Tower clearance.
  • GMC shall hand aircraft to Tower at appropriate positions to avoid unnecessary stopping.

docs.title: Stands 1–20
docs.text: Runway Taxi Route RWY 36R P,Q or R,N,Q RWY 36L P,Q,A or R,N,Q,A RWY 18L P,N,S or R,N,S RWY 18R P,N,S,Y or R,N,S,Y

docs.title: Main Terminal
docs.text: Stand Range Usage Stands 7–9 Domestic Flights Stands 3–6 International Flights

docs.title: 3.3.1 Restrictions
docs.text:
• Stands 4–8 are certified for heavy aircraft operations, including B747.
  • Remaining stands support medium to heavy aircraft.
  • Stands outside 3–9 are remote stands without direct passenger boarding infrastructure.

docs.title: 3.4 Handoff Procedures
docs.text:
Transfer of control between ATS units shall ensure continuous separation and situational awareness.

Controllers shall not issue clearances beyond their area of responsibility without coordination.

Aircraft may be instructed to hold at suitable intermediate points when required.

docs.title: 4. Aerodrome Procedures
docs.text:
The Aerodrome Controller (ADC) is responsible for all movements on active runways and associated taxiways.

ADC shall ensure safe and efficient IFR arrival and departure separation while also coordinating IFR and VFR operations within the control zone.

docs.title: 4.2 Preferred Runways
docs.text:
Due to CAT III ILS capability, RWY 36R is the preferred runway.

When wind conditions require a runway change combined with wind changes greater than 8 knots, RWY 18L becomes the preferred runway.

docs.title: 4.3.1 Line-up Clearance
docs.text: Aircraft handed over to Tower may be cleared to line up once the runway is confirmed clear.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, via Holding Point Q, line up and wait RWY 36R.

docs.title: Conditional Line-up Clearance
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, behind departing company A320, line up and wait RWY 36R behind.

docs.title: 4.3.2 Takeoff Clearance
docs.text: Aircraft shall be cleared for takeoff once separation standards are met.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, surface winds 290 degrees 10 knots, RWY 36R, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 4.3.3 Separation Requirements
docs.text:
• IFR departures shall comply with standard IFR separation minima.
  • Enhanced wake turbulence separation (eWTS) shall be applied where applicable.
  • Aircraft on the same SID shall generally receive a minimum spacing of two minutes unless reduced separation is permitted.
  • VFR departures may be cleared maintaining visual separation where appropriate.

docs.title: 4.3.4 Departure Handoff Procedure
docs.text: Aircraft shall normally be handed to Approach after becoming airborne and reaching 1000 ft AGL.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, airborne time 17, contact Lahore Approach 121.300.

docs.title: 4.3.5 Omni-directional Departures
docs.text: Aircraft conducting omni-directional departures may receive heading assignments after departure to assist with sequencing and terrain separation.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, after departure fly heading 270, maintain altitude 2000 ft, wind 280 degrees 6 knots, RWY 36R, cleared for takeoff.

docs.title: 4.3.6 Rejecting a Departure
docs.text: Aircraft may be instructed to reject takeoff where required to prevent unsafe situations.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, cancel takeoff clearance, stop immediately, I say again, stop immediately.

docs.title: 4.4 Arrival Procedures
docs.text: While Approach primarily manages arrival separation, ADC shall ensure separation is maintained until the preceding aircraft crosses the threshold safely.

docs.title: 4.4.1 Speed Restriction
docs.text: ADC may issue speed control instructions to maintain arrival spacing.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Controller: PIA304, surface winds 290 degrees 08 knots, RWY 36R, continue approach number 2, reduce to minimum approach speed for separation.

docs.title: 4.4.2 Visual Separation
docs.text: Aircraft without Mode C or VFR aircraft may be instructed to maintain visual separation.

docs.title: 4.4.3 Go-around Instruction
docs.text: Controllers may issue go-around instructions where landing is no longer suitable.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Controller: PIA304, go around, I say again, go around, acknowledge going around.

Pilot: PIA304 going around.

Controller: PIA304, continue missed approach procedure, contact Lahore Approach 121.300.

docs.title: 4.4.4 Arrival Taxi Procedure
docs.text: After vacating the runway, aircraft shall receive taxi instructions and be transferred to Ground once fully clear of the runway environment.

docs.title: 4.5 VFR Procedures
docs.text: ADC is responsible for managing all VFR traffic within the control zone.

docs.title: 4.5.1 VFR Departures
docs.text:
Once ready and traffic conditions permit, VFR aircraft shall be cleared for departure in an orderly sequence.

Aircraft shall report when leaving the control zone.

docs.title: 4.5.2 VFR Traffic in Circuit
docs.text:
At Lahore, circuit operations are conducted west of the airfield.

Aircraft shall:

  • Enter downwind as instructed
  • State intentions
  • Expect orbit instructions during IFR arrival sequencing where required

Once orbiting is no longer required, ADC shall issue base turn instructions followed by landing or touch-and-go clearance.

docs.title: 4.5.3 VFR Arrivals
docs.text:
Inbound VFR aircraft shall establish contact with ADC before entering the control zone.

ADC shall issue:

  • Joining instructions
  • Circuit direction
  • Circuit altitude
  • Runway in use
  • Traffic information
  • Sequencing requirements

Pilots shall remain outside controlled airspace until clearance to enter has been issued.

docs.title: 1.1 Purpose
docs.text: This policy defines the local procedures used by Pakistan vACC for controller authorization, training, rating progression, endorsements, visiting controllers, transfers, activity requirements and operational standards within Pakistan vACC airspace on the VATSIM network. The policy is intended to provide a clear, and fair framework for resident, transferring and visiting controllers while preserving the ability of Pakistan vACC to maintain safe, realistic and high-quality online ATC operations.

docs.title: 1.2 Limitation of Liability
docs.text: This document has been created solely for use on the VATSIM network in support of aviation simulation. It must not be used for real-world aviation, training, navigation, or operational purposes. Pakistan vACC, VATWA and VATSIM accept no responsibility for misuse outside the VATSIM simulation environment.

docs.title: 1.3 General Provisions
docs.text:
• All Pakistan vACC controllers must comply with the VATSIM Code of Conduct, GCAP, TVCP, VATWA policies, Pakistan vACC procedures and any published local operating procedures.
  • Pakistan vACC may only restrict access to positions, ratings, training or endorsements where permitted by GCAP, TVCP or higher-level VATSIM/VATWA policy.
  • If any part of this policy conflicts with VATSIM GCAP, VATSIM TVCP, the VATSIM Code of Conduct or VATWA policy, the higher-level policy shall prevail.
  • Policy changes shall be communicated through official Pakistan vACC and/or VATWA channels. Controllers are expected to remain aware of current policy and procedural revisions.

docs.title: 1.4 Acknowledgement
docs.text: Pakistan vACC acknowledges the work of previous Pakistan vACC leadership and training staff who prepared earlier versions of the ATC policy and training material. This updated draft builds upon Rev 1/2025 and restructures it for clearer GCAP/TVCP alignment.

docs.title: 2.1 Authority
docs.text: Pakistan vACC is responsible for administering local ATC training and controller authorization within its delegated area, subject to oversight by VATWA, the relevant VATSIM Region and the VATSIM Board of Governors policy framework. Where GCAP or TVCP assigns approval authority to the Division, Regional Vice President or another higher authority, Pakistan vACC shall not enforce the relevant local restriction until the necessary approval has been obtained and the restriction has been published in the required manner.

docs.title: 2.2 Scope
docs.text:
• Resident controllers allocated to Pakistan vACC.
  • Controllers transferring into Pakistan vACC.
  • Visiting controllers authorized to control within Pakistan vACC airspace.
  • Mentors, instructors, examiners and staff involved in ATC training and administration.
  • ATC positions and endorsements within Pakistan vACC airspace.

docs.title: 2.3 Precedence
docs.text: The order of precedence is: VATSIM Code of Regulations and Code of Conduct; VATSIM GCAP and TVCP; VATWA regional/divisional policy; this Pakistan vACC ATS Policy; local SOPs, LOAs and training documents. Local SOPs and training documents may explain procedures but must not create additional eligibility restrictions beyond this policy and GCAP/TVCP.

docs.title: 3. Definitions
docs.text: Term Meaning in this Policy ATC Rating A VATSIM controller rating: OBS, S1, S2, S3, C1 or C3. ATC Roster A published list showing which members are permitted to control within Pakistan vACC and which endorsements or solo privileges they hold. CPT / Practical Examination A practical assessment of a controller's ability to meet rating or endorsement competencies. Designated Position A Tier 1, Tier 2 or Super Center position requiring an endorsement under GCAP. Endorsement Authorization to operate a position or group of positions requiring knowledge or competency above the base rating. Local Induction Plan A transfer or visitor familiarization pathway covering Pakistan vACC documentation, procedures and any permitted theory or practical elements. Mentor A person authorized by Pakistan vACC/VATWA to conduct training sessions. Solo Endorsement Temporary authorization for a trainee rated S2 or above to control a specified position one rating above their current rating while training for the next rating. Unrestricted Position A position that does not require a Tier 1, Tier 2 or Super Center endorsement and may be controlled by a rostered controller with the required rating. Visitor A controller permitted to control in Pakistan vACC while retaining a home allocation elsewhere.

docs.title: 4.1 VATSIM ATC Ratings
docs.text: Pakistan vACC recognizes the VATSIM ATC rating structure: Observer (OBS), Developing Controller (S1), Aerodrome Controller (S2), Terminal Controller (S3), Enroute Controller (C1) and Senior Controller (C3). Ratings are granted sequentially in accordance with GCAP rating competencies and VATWA processes.

docs.title: 4.2 Minimum Rating Required for Independent Control
docs.text: Minimum Rating Position Type Common Callsign Suffix S1 Clearance Delivery _DEL S1 Ground Control / Ground Sequencing _GND / _DEL S1 Ramp Control, where locally implemented and approved _RMP S2 Tower Control _TWR S3 Approach Control _APP S3 Departure Control _DEP C1 Enroute / FSS / Oceanic where applicable _CTR / _FSS Defined locally Radio Operator / Traffic Flow positions, where implemented _RDO / _TMU / _FMP

docs.title: 4.3 Authorization to Operate
docs.text:
A controller may operate a Pakistan vACC position only when all of the following are satisfied:

  • the controller is listed on the Pakistan vACC or VATWA ATC roster as permitted to control within Pakistan vACC airspace;
  • the controller holds the minimum ATC rating required for the position;
  • where the position is designated, the controller holds the required endorsement or a valid solo endorsement covering that position; and
  • the controller is current and not subject to a valid restriction under this policy, GCAP, TVCP or VATWA policy. Top-down coverage shall apply in accordance with GCAP and local sectorization. A controller providing top down coverage does not need the endorsement for an underlying designated position while another endorsed controller is online on that underlying or adjacent position. If the endorsed controller logs off and the top-down controller lacks the required endorsement, the top-down controller must cease providing that designated service or log onto an appropriate position.

docs.title: 4.4 S1 Tower Authorization
docs.text: Pakistan vACC may only permit Developing Controllers (S1) to operate specified Tower positions independently where VATWA/Division approval exists and the approved positions are clearly published. Unless such approval and publication exists, S1 controllers are limited to S1-level positions except when receiving supervised training or holding a valid solo endorsement for the next rating.

docs.title: 5.1 Unrestricted Positions
docs.text: Any Pakistan vACC position not published as Tier 1, Tier 2 or Super Center is Unrestricted. A rostered controller with the required rating may operate an Unrestricted position without an additional endorsement.

docs.title: 5.2 Tier 1 Designation
docs.text: Tier 1 designation may only be used for positions with high traffic levels and/or complex procedures where incorrect or inefficient operation would significantly affect pilots or adjacent controllers. A Tier 1 endorsement is required to operate the relevant position. Tier 1 training and assessment may include written and practical elements, but shall not exceed the minimum standard needed to operate the position during normal VATSIM operations.

docs.title: 5.3 Tier 2 Designation
docs.text: Tier 2 designation may only be used for positions or groups of positions with significant differences from standard operating procedures. Tier 2 positions must be grouped by a common atypical procedure, and one Tier 2 endorsement shall cover all positions within that group. Tier 2 endorsement training may include self study, limited familiarization, limited simulated or live training and a written exam. A practical exam must not be required for a Tier 2 endorsement.

docs.title: 5.4 Super Center Designation
docs.text: Pakistan vACC shall not operate a Super Center designation unless approved through the GCAP approval process. If approved, the Super Center, its procedures, approval date, endorsement requirements and roster visibility shall be published before enforcement.

docs.title: 5.5 Approval and Publication
docs.text:
• Pakistan vACC shall petition VATWA for any new or changed Tier 1 or Tier 2 designation and shall provide justification showing how the designation meets GCAP.
  • Designated status shall only be enforceable after approval by the competent authority and publication on the Pakistan vACC or VATWA website/roster.
  • The published list must show the designated position or group, the endorsement required, the effective date and the controllers authorized to operate the position or group.
  • Any change that materially alters the scope of a designation shall be reviewed through the appropriate Division/Region process before enforcement.

docs.title: 6. ATC Roster and Publication Requirements
docs.text: Pakistan vACC shall maintain or link to an ATC roster available to VATSIM account holders. The roster shall identify which controllers are authorized to control in Pakistan vACC airspace and, where applicable, the endorsements they hold. The roster shall include, as applicable: controller name, VATSIM CID, home/visitor/transfer status, rating, authorized positions or position groups, Tier 1/Tier 2/Super Center endorsements, solo endorsements with expiry dates, and any published restrictions relevant to position authorization. Solo endorsement expiry dates and designated position endorsements must be visible before they are enforced. Pakistan vACC staff shall update the roster as soon as practicable after rating upgrades, endorsement awards, removals, visitor approvals, transfer completions or reinstatements.

docs.title: 7.1 General Training Principles
docs.text:
• Training shall focus on the GCAP rating competencies, Pakistan vACC local procedures, coordination standards, phraseology and practical readiness for normal VATSIM traffic.
  • Training requirements shall not exceed what is necessary to meet the competencies for the relevant rating on Unrestricted positions, except where a separate endorsement is being sought.
  • Resident members shall request training through the official Pakistan HQ/training system.
  • Mentors and instructors shall record training sessions, trainee progress, recommendations and deficiencies in the approved training record system.
  • Trainees are expected to prepare using current Pakistan vACC manuals, SOPs, charts, sector files and any required VATWA e-learning material.

docs.title: 7.2 Written Examinations
docs.text: Written examinations shall assess rating knowledge and local procedure knowledge where appropriate. A score of not less than 90% is required to pass any written examination used for rating progression or endorsement issuance. Where an exam is failed, Pakistan vACC/VATWA may impose a cooling-off period to allow further study. The first written examination cooling-off period shall not exceed 72 hours. Any extended period after repeated failures shall follow VATWA policy and must remain reasonable and training-focused.

docs.title: 7.3 Practical Training
docs.text: Practical training may be conducted on sweatbox/simulator sessions or the live VATSIM network under an approved mentor/instructor. When training on the live network, the mentor must be connected, observing relevant communications and able to intervene where required. Practical training sessions are not themselves practical examinations unless the trainee has been clearly informed that the session is an assessment and the approved assessment criteria are being used.

docs.title: 8.1 OBS to S1 - Developing Controller
docs.text:
1. Member joins or is allocated to Pakistan vACC and reviews the induction material.
  2. Member studies relevant S1 material covering clearance delivery, ground control, phraseology, basic coordination, flight plans, METAR/TAF interpretation and client setup.
  3. Member passes the required vACC and the divisional written examination with at least 90%.
  4. Upon completion of VATWA/Pakistan vACC administrative requirements, the S1 rating/authorization is processed, and the member may be rostered for S1-level positions.
  5. A practical familiarization session may be offered or recommended before the first live ATC session, but a practical exam is not required for the S1 rating unless higher-level policy changes.

docs.title: 8.2 S1 to S2 - Aerodrome Controller
docs.text:
1. Trainee meets any VATWA/Pakistan vACC approved consolidation or prerequisite requirement for S2 training.
  2. Trainee studies tower control material including runway selection, departures, arrivals, circuit/VFR operations, wake turbulence, missed approaches/go-arounds, coordination and local aerodrome procedures.
  3. Trainee completes practical training with an approved mentor/instructor.
  4. Trainee passes the required written examination with at least 90%.
  5. Trainee may receive a valid S2 solo endorsement for specified _T_TWR position(s), where appropriate, for additional practice.
  6. Trainee undertakes and passes an approved S2 practical examination/CPT assessing S2 competencies on an Unrestricted Tower position or an approved equivalent pathway.
  7. The S2 rating is recommended and processed as soon as practicable after successful completion.

docs.title: 8.3 S2 to S3 - Terminal Controller
docs.text:
1. Trainee meets any VATWA/Pakistan vACC approved consolidation or prerequisite requirement for S3 training.
  2. Trainee studies terminal procedures including arrival/departure control, airspace boundaries, separation, vectoring, speed/altitude control, approach clearances, holding, missed approach coordination and adjacent sector coordination.
  3. The trainee completes practical training with an approved mentor/instructor.
  4. Trainee passes the required written examination with at least 90%.
  5. Trainee may receive a valid solo endorsement for specified _T_APP/_T_DEP position(s), where appropriate.
  6. Trainee undertakes and passes an approved S3 practical examination/CPT assessing S3 competencies on an Unrestricted terminal position or an approved equivalent pathway.
  7. The S3 rating is recommended and processed as soon as practicable after successful completion.

docs.title: 8.4 S3 to C1 - Enroute Controller
docs.text:
1. Trainee meets any VATWA/Pakistan vACC approved consolidation or prerequisite requirement for C1 training.
  2. Trainee studies enroute procedures including sector boundaries, top-down responsibilities, route structures, coordination, sequencing, surveillance/non-surveillance procedures where applicable, weather and traffic information, STAR/descent planning and inter-facility coordination.
  3. Trainee completes practical training with an approved mentor/instructor.
  4. Trainee passes the required written examination with at least 90%.
  5. Trainee may receive a valid solo endorsement for specified _T_CTR position(s), where appropriate.
  6. Trainee undertakes and passes an approved C1 practical examination/CPT assessing C1 competencies on an Unrestricted enroute position, or on a designated position only where GCAP permits and the assessment path still allows rating award based on Unrestricted competencies.
  7. The C1 rating is recommended and processed as soon as practicable after successful completion.

docs.title: 8.5 C1 to C3 - Senior Controller
docs.text: The C3 rating does not grant additional ATC operating privileges beyond C1. Any recommendation for C3 shall follow VATSIM/VATWA policy. Pakistan vACC’s ATS Department may submit a recommendation only where the controller demonstrates sustained maturity, professionalism, leadership, procedural knowledge and contribution to the ATC community. Final approval rests with the competent VATWA authority.

docs.title: 8.6 Endorsements and Rating Progression
docs.text: Pakistan vACC must provide a pathway for members to achieve ratings up to S3 that does not require them to also obtain endorsements. If training or an assessment occurs on a designated position, the trainee must remain eligible to receive the rating once the Unrestricted rating competencies are met, even if the trainee does not meet the separate endorsement requirements. Endorsement training or assessment must not delay rating training or rating assessment. Written endorsement training may be conducted alongside rating training, but endorsement decisions shall remain separate from rating decisions.

docs.title: 9. Solo Endorsements
docs.text:
• A solo endorsement may be issued only to a controller rated S1 or higher who is training for the next ATC rating.
  • A solo endorsement authorizes the trainee to independently operate a specific position, or smaller part of that position, one rating above their current rating.
  • Solo endorsements shall be issued for a continuous period between 7 and 90 days. The total solo endorsement time at one rating shall not exceed 90 days unless a Region Vice President approves an extension permitted by GCAP.
  • A solo endorsement must not be issued merely because practical training is unavailable.
  • Solo endorsement details, including expiry date, trainee name, CID and position, must be published on the Pakistan vACC/VATWA roster before use.
  • A solo endorsement may cover a Tier 1 or Tier 2 position only where approved by the Division and only for the specific position(s) listed. Typical solo endorsements in Pakistan vACC may include S1-to-S2 tower solo, S2-to-S3 approach/departure solo and S3-to-C1 enroute solo. The exact position(s), duration and restrictions shall be determined by staff based on trainee readiness, traffic expectations, mentor recommendations and GCAP limits.

docs.title: 10.1 Purpose
docs.text: A Controller Practical Test (CPT) or practical examination assesses whether the candidate demonstrates the competencies required for the relevant rating or endorsement. Practical examinations must use specific criteria approved by VATWA/Pakistan vACC as applicable.

docs.title: 10.2 Format
docs.text:
• A practical examination may be conducted on the live VATSIM.
  • Live CPTs may be scheduled during events where appropriate, but the traffic level should allow a fair assessment of normal VATSIM operations and must not be artificially unreasonable.
  • A practical examination for S2, S3 or C1 rating progression shall include the required GCAP rating competencies for that rating.
  • A Tier 1 endorsement practical examination, where used, shall evaluate the additional competencies required for the Tier 1 position. A Tier 2 endorsement shall not require a practical examination.

docs.title: 10.3 Outcomes
docs.text: If a candidate passes, the rating or endorsement recommendation shall be processed as soon as practicable, with the goal of completion within 48 hours where administrative systems allow. If a candidate does not pass, the examiner shall provide clear feedback identifying the criteria or competencies that were deficient. The trainee shall receive a focused remedial training plan before another attempt is scheduled. Failure of a practical examination shall not be used punitively and shall not remove an existing rating unless a separate GCAP standards process is followed.

docs.title: 11.1 Consolidation Between Ratings
docs.text: A consolidation period after a rating upgrade may only be required where permitted by VATWA/Division policy. Any consolidation requirement shall not exceed 50 hours and shall not be limited to an area smaller than Pakistan vACC. Hours shall only be counted when the rating being consolidated is required for the position controlled. Participation in local events is encouraged because it improves coordination and traffic handling. However, event participation shall not be used as a mandatory additional prerequisite for rating training unless expressly permitted by VATWA/Division policy and GCAP.

docs.title: 11.2 Position Signoff
docs.text: Pakistan vACC may use local signoff procedures to confirm that a member has reviewed local SOPs, sector files, coordination procedures and position-specific expectations. A signoff shall not be used to create an additional rating restriction beyond GCAP unless tied to an approved endorsement, roster currency requirement or VATWA-approved training process.

docs.title: 11.3 Returning After a Long Break
docs.text: A controller returning after a long period of inactivity may be asked to complete a currency/reinstatement plan before being restored to the roster or before further training. Unless the controller requests additional training, the plan shall be limited to relevant self-study/written material and/or one practical session with a suitable controller, in line with GCAP currency reinstatement rules.

docs.title: 12.1 Eligibility
docs.text:
• A visiting applicant must normally hold S3 or higher and be current in their home allocation.
  • The applicant must have completed at least 50 hours on positions requiring their current rating in their home allocation before applying to visit, unless higher-level policy provides otherwise.
  • A visiting applicant may be rejected only for reasons permitted by TVCP, including ineligibility, disciplinary history within the last year, or failure to meet the visited allocation's published currency requirement assessed within the preceding two quarters.
  • Instructor ratings are for use within the home allocation and do not authorize a visitor to log on as an instructor in Pakistan vACC.

docs.title: 12.2 Application and Initial Authorization
docs.text:
1. Visitor submits an application through Pakistan vACC HQ or the official application process published by VATWA/Pakistan vACC.
  2. Pakistan vACC verifies eligibility, rating, home allocation currency and any relevant disciplinary/currency information.
  3. Visitor completes the required local induction: documentation review, local procedure study and any permitted theory assessment or familiarization session.
  4. Pakistan vACC may require the visitor to obtain visiting status on Unrestricted positions and complete up to 10 hours on those positions before seeking designated position endorsements.
  5. Once approved, the visitor is added to the published visitor roster showing authorized positions and endorsements.

docs.title: 12.3 Visitor Training and Endorsements
docs.text: Visitor training and assessment shall not exceed the minimum standard needed to operate the requested position(s) during normal VATSIM operations. Visitors may apply for Unrestricted and Designated positions. Pakistan vACC may prioritize home members for ratings and endorsements but must not make it impossible or impractical for visitors to obtain visitor status or endorsements. Visitors cannot receive training for new ATC ratings in Pakistan vACC unless Pakistan vACC is their home allocation. Visitors may, however, receive local procedure familiarization and endorsement training required to control within Pakistan vACC.

docs.title: 12.4 Visitor Currency
docs.text:
• Visitors must perform at least half of their controlling in their home allocation in each quarter.
  • Visitors must also meet Pakistan vACC's published roster currency requirement while on the Pakistan visitor roster.
  • A visitor who is no longer current in their home allocation may be removed from the Pakistan vACC visiting roster.
  • A visitor removed for currency may request reinstatement when current in their home allocation. First reinstatement shall follow the same currency reinstatement process as home members. If the visitor repeatedly loses currency within two years or does not request return within six months, Pakistan vACC may require a fresh visiting application.

docs.title: 13.1 Eligibility and Process
docs.text:
• Transfers are governed by VATSIM TVCP and relevant VATWA/Pakistan vACC procedures.
  • A controller with an ATC rating must normally have completed at least 50 hours on positions requiring their current rating in their home allocation before requesting transfer.
  • A transfer request is normally not permitted until 90 days after a new rating award, a completed transfer, or return to a previous allocation after an incomplete transfer.
  • The transfer period is normally 90 days from approval/notification, subject to TVCP extensions where applicable.
  • A transfer request may only be rejected for reasons permitted by TVCP and, where required, with Division/sub-division agreement and written reasons.

docs.title: 13.2 Local Induction Plan
docs.text: For a transfer into Pakistan vACC from within the same Division, the Local Induction Plan shall not be more restrictive than procedure familiarization, documentation review, a theory assessment and/or up to 10 hours of unsupervised controlling for familiarity. Practical training and practical assessment shall not be required for intra-division transfers except permitted enroute familiarization sessions. For a transfer from another Division or Region, the Local Induction Plan may include a Competency Check on the live network where permitted by TVCP. Practical training may be offered or recommended, but the transferring controller may elect to proceed directly to the Competency Check where TVCP allows. The transfer is completed when the controller can operate Unrestricted positions up to their current rating in Pakistan vACC. On completion, Pakistan vACC/VATWA shall make the necessary member record and roster updates.

docs.title: 13.3 Enroute Familiarization
docs.text: Because local enroute procedures can significantly affect adjacent controllers and pilots, Pakistan vACC may require familiarization training for visitors or transferees seeking to control Pakistan vACC enroute positions. Familiarization may include a short theory course, a theory exam based on local documentation or limited practical familiarization. It shall focus only on local procedures and shall not be treated as a rating competency assessment.

docs.title: 14.1 Activity Requirement
docs.text:
Controller Status Requirement Measurement Period Resident 6 hours controlling on Pakistan vACC controllable positions Semi-annual / 6-month period Visitor 3 hours controlling on Pakistan vACC controllable positions, plus home allocation currency and 50% home controlling requirement Per quarter / 3-month period

The activity requirement shall be applied uniformly to controllers of the same status and rating. Pakistan vACC may issue reminders before roster review dates. Removal for activity/currency is administrative and does not remove the member's VATSIM ATC rating.

docs.title: 14.2 Procedure Currency
docs.text: Controllers are expected to remain current with significant local procedure, policy, sectorization and LOA changes. Where Pakistan vACC requires acknowledgement of procedure changes, the controller shall not be considered out of date until the change has been outstanding for more than two months. Before removal based on failure to acknowledge a procedure update, Pakistan vACC shall notify the controller and provide one month to re-establish currency.

docs.title: 14.3 Roster Removal and Reinstatement
docs.text: A controller who does not meet currency requirements may be removed from the roster and made ineligible for further rating/endorsement training until reinstated. Reinstatement may be immediate where staff are satisfied that the controller remains current, or may require a focused currency plan. Unless the controller requests more, the plan shall be no more extensive than self-study/written material and/or one practical session with a suitable controller.

docs.title: 14.4 Endorsement Currency
docs.text:
• Tier 1 or Super Center endorsement currency may use an activity requirement that includes endorsed positions and positions capable of controlling the endorsed airspace top-down.
  • Tier 2 endorsement currency may only be based on acknowledgement of relevant procedure changes, not a separate activity-hours requirement.
  • An endorsement removed for currency may be reinstated immediately or through a focused plan no more restrictive than the original endorsement requirement or the GCAP currency reinstatement process.

docs.title: 15. ATC Sessions, Top-Down Coverage and Operational Conduct
15.1 Session Expectations
docs.text:
Controllers should plan ATC sessions of sufficient length to provide a stable and useful service. The following session durations are recommended for planning and quality of service purposes:

ATC Position Recommended Minimum Session Delivery 45 minutes Ground 45 minutes Tower 45 minutes Approach/Departure 1 hour Enroute 1 hour

Shorter sessions may be reasonable due to real-life commitments, traffic levels or controller workload. These recommendations do not override GCAP or roster currency rules.

docs.title: 15.2 Before Logging On
docs.text:
• Review current NOTAM-like vACC notices, active sectorization, adjacent ATC, runway configuration and weather.
  • Coordinate with active adjacent or underlying controllers before connecting where required.
  • Ensure controller client, audio client, sector files and plugins are current and functioning.
  • When joining during busy traffic, consider observing briefly before opening to avoid disrupting ongoing coordination.

docs.title: 15.3 Top-Down Service and Workload
docs.text: Controllers shall provide top-down coverage in accordance with GCAP, local SOPs and reasonable workload limits. Where a controller becomes overloaded, the controller may reduce top-down service or request pilots to expect delays. In extreme cases, the controller may withdraw top-down service from an airport or move to a smaller/split position where available.

docs.title: 15.4 Events
docs.text: Event rosters may be managed separately from ATC roster privileges. Failure to attend an accepted event booking may result in event-roster consequences, de-prioritization for future events or staff follow-up. Any removal of general ATC privileges must still comply with GCAP, TVCP and this policy.

docs.title: 16. Mentors, Instructors and Training Records
docs.text:
• Mentors and instructors shall be appointed or authorized under Pakistan vACC procedures.
  • Mentors may provide theory, sweatbox, live-network and familiarization training within the scope of their authorization.
  • Instructors/examiners shall conduct practical examinations only where authorized and using approved criteria.
  • Training records shall be clear, objective, respectful and useful for the trainee's next session.
  • A trainee may request clarification of feedback or a training decision through the ATC Training Director or Pakistan vACC Director. Escalation to VATWA shall follow VATWA procedures.

docs.title: 17. Standards, Restrictions and Removal of Privileges
docs.text:
Pakistan vACC's primary goal is to keep suitably rated controllers active and supported. Restricting or removing controlling privileges should be a last resort and shall follow GCAP/TVCP requirements.

  • Where a controller appears below standard, staff should first attempt to resolve the matter through feedback, mentoring, self-study or procedure clarification.
  • Where a controller is consistently below the required standard to the point that it disrupts pilots or other controllers, Pakistan vACC may petition VATWA for restrictions in accordance with GCAP.
  • Any petition must be supported by reliable and sustained evidence and must be reviewed through the Division/Region process required by GCAP.
  • Possible restrictions may include removal of endorsements, removal from the roster, or ineligibility for training until the issue is resolved, where approved by the competent authority.
  • Disciplinary matters shall be handled under VATSIM/VATWA processes. This policy does not replace the VATSIM Code of Conduct or supervisor processes.

docs.title: 18. References
docs.text: Reference Document / Policy VATSIM-POL-GCAP VATSIM Global Controller Administration Policy, Version 1.1 VATSIM-POL-TVCP VATSIM Transfer and Visiting Controller Policy, Version 2.0 VATSIM Code of Conduct Current VATSIM member conduct policy VATWA Policy / HQ Procedures Applicable VATWA division/subdivision training, rostering and ticketing procedures Pakistan vACC ATC Manual / SOPs / LOAs Local procedures, sectorization, coordination and operational guidance

docs.title: Appendix A. Position Designation Implementation List
docs.text: The following implementation list reflects the designations carried forward from the previous Pakistan vACC policy draft. Pakistan vACC must verify VATWA/Region approval and publish the approved list on the website/roster before enforcing any designated position restriction. If the official approved designated-position list differs from this appendix, the official approved list prevails.

docs.title: A.1 Tier 1 Positions
docs.text: Position / Airspace Designation Endorsement Implementation Note CHR_APP Tier 1 CHR_APP Tier 1 Complex terrain/procedure environment. Enforce only if approved and published. OPLR_CTR Tier 1 OPLR_CTR Tier 1 Complex enroute/terminal top-down environment. Enforce only if approved and published. ISB_CTR Tier 1 ISB_CTR Tier 1 Complex sector/traffic mix. Enforce only if approved and published. OPLR-W_CTR Tier 1 OPLR-W_CTR Tier 1 Sector split/complex coordination. Enforce only if approved and published.

docs.title: A.2 Tier 2 Groups
docs.text: Group Likely Positions / Airfields Endorsement Permitted Training/Assessment Northern Airfields OPSD, OPGT, OPCH, OPSS and any other published northern/mountain airfields Northern Airfields Tier 2 Study + optional familiarization + written exam. No practical exam. Multan OPMT and related published positions Multan Tier 2 Study + optional familiarization + written exam. No practical exam. Quetta OPQT and related published positions Quetta Tier 2 Study + optional familiarization + written exam. No practical exam. Peshawar OPPS and related published positions Peshawar Tier 2 Study + optional familiarization + written exam. No practical exam.

docs.title: A.3 Unrestricted Positions
docs.text: All other Pakistan vACC positions are Unrestricted unless and until approved and published as Designated Positions. Unrestricted status does not remove the requirement for the controller to hold the minimum ATC rating and be listed on the roster.

docs.title: Appendix B. Minimum Rating Competency Summary
docs.text: This appendix summarizes the competency areas used by Pakistan vACC training. The controlling authority remains GCAP Appendix A and any VATWA-approved assessment criteria. Rating Core Competency Areas S1 DEL/GND role, phraseology, flight plans, weather, basic airspace, controller client setup, clearances, taxi/ground movement, runway crossing coordination and basic coordination. S2 Tower role, runway configuration, takeoff/landing clearances, VFR circuits, separation, wake turbulence, go-arounds/missed approaches, runway changes and coordination. S3 APP/DEP roles, airspace boundaries, radar identification, vectoring, speed/altitude control, separation, approach clearances, holding, uncontrolled operations and coordination. C1 C3 Enroute role, sector management, multiple frequencies/transceivers, advanced scan, coordination, sequencing, separation, STAR/descent clearances, route deviation requests, weather/traffic services. No additional operating privilege beyond C1; awarded under VATSIM/VATWA criteria for senior controller recognition.

docs.title: Appendix C. Implementation Notes and Changes from Rev 1/2025
docs.text:
• Tier 2 Practical Exams Removed: Tier 2 endorsements may not require a practical exam. The updated policy limits Tier 2 checks to self-study, limited familiarization/training and written examination.
  • Visitor Eligibility Updated: The previous 200-hour S3 visitor requirement has been replaced with TVCP aligned eligibility, including S3+ current status and 50 hours on current-rating positions in the home allocation.
  • Transfers Clarified: Intra-division transfers are limited to documentation, theory and up to 10 hours unsupervised familiarization, with no practical assessment except permitted enroute familiarization.
  • Currency Reinstatement Limited: Returning controllers cannot be required to repeat a full rating course unless they request it. Reinstatement is focused on currency, self-study and/or one practical session.
  • Consolidation Reframed: The previous 50-hour signoff concept has been retained only as a VATWA approved consolidation maximum. Mandatory event participation has been changed to encouragement rather than a hard rating prerequisite.
  • Roster Visibility Added: The policy now requires publication of controller authorizations, endorsements and solo expiry dates.
  • Rating and Endorsement Decisions Separated: Ratings are awarded on Unrestricted-position competencies even where a separate endorsement is not awarded.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This document establishes the structure, governance, and operational framework of VATSIM Pakistan (vACC). It defines how the division is managed, how decisions are made, and how responsibilities are distributed among staff.

It ensures transparency, accountability, and consistency across all operations within VATSIM Pakistan. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to:

  • Define organizational structure
  • Establish roles and responsibilities
  • Provide governance guidelines
  • Ensure consistent decision-making
  • Maintain alignment with VATSIM global policies

docs.title: Structure of VATSIM Pakistan
docs.text: VATSIM Pakistan operates as a subdivision (vACC) under the VATSIM network.

docs.title: Leadership
docs.text:
The division is managed by appointed staff members responsible for operations, training, and administration.

Typical roles include:

  • Division Director
  • Operations Director
  • Training Director
  • Web / Technical Team
  • Events & Community Team

docs.title: Staff Responsibilities
docs.text:
Staff members are responsible for:

  • Managing ATC operations
  • Conducting training and evaluations
  • Maintaining rosters
  • Handling feedback and support
  • Ensuring policy compliance

docs.title: Member Responsibilities
docs.text:
All members are expected to:

  • Follow VATSIM Code of Conduct
  • Follow VATSIM Code of Regulations
  • Follow Pakistan vACC policies
  • Maintain professional behavior

Global VATSIM policies always take precedence where applicable. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

docs.title: Decision Making
docs.text:
• Decisions are made by designated staff members
  • Major decisions may involve consultation with regional/divisional authorities
  • Policies are reviewed periodically and updated as required

docs.title: Policy Compliance
docs.text:
All members and staff must comply with:

  • VATSIM global policies
  • Pakistan vACC policies
  • Local procedures

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action.

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
Violations may result in:

  • Warnings
  • Removal from roster
  • Suspension of privileges
  • Escalation to VATSIM authorities

docs.title: Communication
docs.text:
Official communication channels include:

  • Website
  • Discord
  • Email

Users are expected to stay updated through these platforms.

docs.title: Amendments
docs.text:
• Policies may be updated periodically
  • Changes are announced through official channels
  • Continued use of services implies acceptance of updates

docs.title: Liability
docs.text:
VATSIM Pakistan is not responsible for:

  • Misuse of simulation procedures
  • Incorrect interpretation of aviation practices
  • Real-world application of simulation content

docs.title: Acknowledgement
docs.text:
By participating in VATSIM Pakistan services, users acknowledge that they:

  • Understand this policy
  • Agree to comply with it
  • Accept governance under VATSIM Pakistan

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This policy defines how VATSIM Pakistan operates as an organized subdivision, ensuring structured management, clear responsibilities, and alignment with global VATSIM standards.

!!! info This policy applies only to simulation on the VATSIM network and not to real-world aviation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This policy outlines how VATSIM Pakistan (vACC) collects, uses, stores, and protects personal data of its users. It ensures compliance with applicable data protection standards, including principles aligned with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

This policy applies only to operations within the VATSIM network and does not apply to real-world aviation.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to:

  • Ensure data protection and privacy compliance
  • Maintain transparency in data usage
  • Protect users and staff
  • Enable smooth operation of VATSIM Pakistan services

docs.title: How We Collect Data
docs.text:
Data is collected through:

  • VATSIM API integrations
  • Website services (e.g. feedback, roster, training systems)
  • Discord and support platforms

By using VATSIM Pakistan services, users consent to the collection and processing of their data.

docs.title: Types of Data Collected
docs.text:
We may collect:

  • Full name and email (from VATSIM account)
  • VATSIM CID and ratings
  • Region, division, subdivision
  • Discord user ID and profile
  • Training and support records
  • Feedback submissions
  • Any additional voluntarily provided data

docs.title: Purpose of Data Usage
docs.text:
Data is collected to:

  • Manage users and authentication
  • Provide training and support services
  • Maintain ATC rosters
  • Enable access to services
  • Facilitate operational and administrative tasks
  • Verify eligibility for fast-track ratings

docs.title: Public Data
docs.text:
The following may be publicly visible:

  • Name and VATSIM CID
  • Rating and training status
  • Solo validations
  • ATC session bookings
  • Live connection status

docs.title: Private Data
docs.text:
Personal data is shared only with:

  • VATSIM staff
  • VATWA (regional authority) staff

No data is shared with unauthorized third parties.

docs.title: Fast-Track Verification
docs.text:
To verify real-world pilots/controllers:

  • Licenses may be submitted
  • Access is restricted to authorized staff only
  • All documents are deleted after verification

Users are responsible for any incorrect submissions.

docs.title: Data Usage for Enforcement
docs.text:
Collected data may be used to:

  • Investigate misconduct
  • Enforce VATSIM Code of Conduct
  • Apply disciplinary actions where necessary

docs.title: Data Storage & Security
docs.text:
• Data is stored in secure systems
  • Access is restricted based on role
  • Access is monitored and audited
  • Encryption and secure protocols are used where applicable

docs.title: Data Retention
docs.text:
• Data is retained only as long as necessary
  • Users may request updates or corrections
  • Data may be removed upon valid request

docs.title: User Rights
docs.text:
Users have the right to:

  • Access their data
  • Request corrections
  • Request deletion where applicable
  • Object to data processing

Requests should be made through official VATSIM channels.

docs.title: Policy Updates
docs.text:
• This policy is reviewed periodically
  • Updates are announced via official channels (Discord, forums, website)
  • The latest version will always be available on the website

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For questions regarding this policy, contact VATSIM Pakistan staff through official communication channels.

!!! info This policy applies only within the VATSIM simulation network and not to real-world aviation operations.
